it: In questa scheda descriveremo una serie di esperimenti realizzati disponendo
en: {0}In this card we will describe a series of experiments carried out by arranging several Stern–Gerlach machines in cascade. The results of these experiments will lead us to introduce the fundamental features of Quantum Mechanics and, in the final part, we will give a first simplified formulation of this theory.

it: (Vedi fig.1) Abbiamo due macchine di Stern-Gerlach in fila
en: (See fig. 1) We have two Stern–Gerlach machines in a row, one with a vertical magnetic axis, the other inclined with respect to the vertical by an angle ϑ.

it: La prima macchina divide il fascio atomico in due parti
en: {0}The first machine splits the atomic beam into two parts; we will write that one part has magnetic moment m0=+k and the other m0=-k. The subscript zero in the symbol m0 represents the inclination angle of the machine; for example, if the angle is 45° we will write m45. The symbol k represents the absolute value of the measured magnetic moment which, as we saw in the previous card, is always the same in every experiment.

it: La figura 2 mostra uno schema che rappresenta la disposizione delle macchine
en: Figure 2 shows a diagram representing the arrangement of the machines and the path of the atoms. At the output of the first machine the beam with m0=-k is blocked and only the beam with m0=+k is let through; we will therefore say that the first machine selects atoms with m0=+k.

it: Il fascio con m0=+k, viene fatto passare nella seconda macchina
en: The beam with m0=+k is passed into the second machine and here it splits again into two parts, mϑ=+k and mϑ=-k. The two fractions are in general not equal and it is seen experimentally that they are proportional to the terms .

it: Ad esempio se anche la seconda macchina è disposta verticalmente (fig.
en: For example, if the second machine is also placed vertically (fig. {0} ϑ=0°) then the beams mϑ=+k and mϑ=-k have intensities proportional to the terms 1 and 0, that is the beam mϑ=+k has all the intensity and the beam mϑ=-k has zero intensity. If instead the second machine is placed at ninety degrees (fig. 4 ϑ=90°) then the beams m90=+k and m90=-k have intensities proportional to the terms ½ and ½, that is they are equal.

it: E' interessante osservare che già questi primi risultati non si possono spiegare
en: It is interesting to observe that already these first results cannot be explained by the concepts of classical mechanics.

it: Se ruotiamo anche la prima macchina (fig.5) osserviamo che i risultati non
en: If we also rotate the first machine (fig. 5) we observe that the results do not change substantially and, as was easy to imagine, the two outgoing beams have intensities proportional to the terms

it: Questo vuol dire che ciò che conta è solo l'inclinazione relativa tra le due
en: This means that what matters is only the relative inclination between the two machines and not the absolute inclination with respect to the plumb line.

it: (Vedi fig.6) Abbiamo tre macchine. Consideriamo il fascio uscente dalla mac
en: (See fig. 6) We have three machines. Let us consider the beam coming out of machine two; this beam is split by machine three into two parts of intensities proportional to the terms . If the inclination of the first machine is varied, we observe that the intensity of the beam coming out of the second machine varies, but the way in which the third machine splits this beam does not vary, that is the ratios between the intensities of the outgoing beams and the intensity of the beam entering the third machine do not vary. This experiment leads us to conclude that the atoms coming out of machine two are in a physical state that is independent of the orientation of machine one. We will indicate this physical state by saying that the atoms are in the state mϑ2=+k.

it: Interpretazione probabilistica dei risultati.
en: Probabilistic interpretation of the results.

it: In questo paragrafo daremo una possibile interpretazione di queste prime es
en: In this section we will give a possible interpretation of these first experiences that we have described.

it: Consideriamo il primo esperimento, la cui figura è riportata qui per comodi
en: {0}Let us consider the first experiment, whose figure is reproduced here for convenience. We can think that machine one places the atoms in a particular physical state, which we will denote by m0=+k. If a measurement of m0 is performed on these atoms, orienting machine two vertically, the value m0=+k is obtained with certainty. If instead a measurement of mϑ (with ϑ≠0) is performed by inclining the machine, one obtains that each single atom has a probability {1} of giving mϑ=+k, and a probability {2} of giving mϑ=-k.

it: In altre parole la nostra interpretazione suona così:
en: In other words, our interpretation reads as follows:

it: se, su un atomo che si trova nello stato m0=+k, si misura la grandezza mϑ
en: if, on an atom in the state m0=+k, the quantity mϑ (projection of the magnetic moment in the direction ϑ) is measured, two possible outcomes occur: mϑ=+k, with probability {0}, and mϑ=-k with probability {1}.

it: Secondo questa interpretazione il fascio uscente dalla prima macchina non è
en: According to this interpretation, the beam coming out of the first machine is not composed of a fraction of atoms with mϑ=+k and another with mϑ=-k, but consists of atoms that are all in the state m0=+k and each of them, when mϑ is measured, has a certain probability of giving mϑ=+k and another of giving mϑ=-k.

it: Questo modo di vedere le cose, forse non è l'unico possibile, ma è sicurame
en: This way of seeing things is perhaps not the only possible one, but it is certainly very difficult to imagine another view consistent with all the facts we have described and will describe.

it: A questo punto siamo arrivati a delineare una prima importante caratteristi
en: At this point we have outlined a first important feature of Quantum Mechanics, the one linked to probability. When a measurement is performed on a physical system several outcomes are possible, in our case two, but there may be more or even infinitely many. Each possible outcome has a certain probability of occurring, and Quantum Mechanics will allow us to compute these probabilities.

it: Sovrapposizione degli stati.
en: Superposition of states.

it: La figura 7 mostra che con due macchine di Stern-Gerlach è possibile sovrap
en: Figure 7 shows that with two Stern–Gerlach machines it is possible to recombine two beams that were previously separated by a first machine. The experiments of this section are based on the superposition of two beams.

it: (Vedi fig.8) La prima macchina dispone gli atomi nello stato m0=+k, la seco
en: (See fig. 8) The first machine places the atoms in the state m0=+k, the second splits the beam into two parts having m90=+k and m90=-k, and the last two recombine the beam.

it: Si eseguono tre misure di m0 (fig.9).
en: Three measurements of m0 are performed (fig. 9).

it: Nel caso 1 misuriamo m0 su atomi nello stato m90=+k
en: {0}In case 1 we measure m0 on atoms in the state m90=+k, so we have the two possibilities m0=+k and m0=-k with probabilities 0.5 and 0.5.

it: Nel caso 2 misuriamo m0 su atomi nello stato m90=-k abbiamo ancora le due p
en: In case 2 we measure m0 on atoms in the state m90=-k; we again have the two possibilities m0=+k with probability 0.5 and m0=-k with probability 0.5.

it: Nel caso 3 misuriamo m0 su atomi che si trovano in uno stato che è sovrappo
en: In case 3 we measure m0 on atoms that are in a state which is a superposition of the states m90=+k and m90=-k. The result is that we have the two possibilities m0=+k with probability 1 and m0=-k with probability 0, exactly as if there had been no splitting and recombination.

it: Riassumendo abbiamo:
en: To summarise, we have:

it: La cosa da osservare è che sovrapponendo due stati che hanno probabilità 0,
en: The thing to note is that by superposing two states that each have probability 0.5 of giving m0=-k one obtains a state that has zero probability of having m0=-k,

it: Quindi sovrapponendo gli stati le probabilità non si sommano. Ci chiediamo
en: So by superposing the states the probabilities do not add. We ask whether there is some other mathematical entity linked to the physical states that, when the states are superposed, combines according to a simple law, perhaps a law of addition.

it: (Vedi fig.10) Questo esperimento è quasi identico al precedente, con l'unic
en: (See fig. 10) This experiment is almost identical to the previous one, with the only difference that the beam with m90=-k travels a longer path than the beam with m90=+k. This can be achieved with the system represented schematically in figure 11. The lower beam passes through a region where the field gradient is more intense, so it curves more and travels a longer path.

it: Vediamo quali sono i risultati al variare dello sfasamento
en: {0}Let us see what the results are as the phase shift ∆ between the two paths varies.

it: Se lo sfasamento è nullo in uscita abbiamo lo stato m0=+k esattamente come
en: If the phase shift is zero, at the output we have the state m0=+k exactly as in the previous experiment, but if the phase shift is different from zero we have a different state. If ∆ becomes equal to a certain characteristic length λ, at the output we again have m0=+k; even if ∆ is a multiple of λ, ∆=2λ,3λ, etc., we still have the state m0=+k. If ∆ is equal to λ/2, at the output we have the state m0=-k!

it: In generale per le probabilità abbiamo le seguenti formule:
en: In general, for the probabilities we have the following formulas:

it: dove abbiamo introdotto la variabile ϕ=2π∆/λ.
en: where we have introduced the variable ϕ=2π∆/λ.

it: Ora facciamo i seguenti calcoli matematici.
en: Now let us make the following mathematical calculations.

it: Prendiamo due numeri nel campo complesso, ad esempio due volte il numero un
en: {0}Let us take two numbers in the complex field, for example the number one twice, 1 and 1, and represent them as vectors

it: Ora sfasiamo il secondo numero moltiplicandolo per il fasore
en: {0}Now we phase-shift the second number by multiplying it by the phasor eiϕ, obtaining the pair 1 and eiϕ

it: Sommiamo il risultato così ottenuto e prendiamone il modulo quadrato
en: {0}We add the result thus obtained and take its squared modulus

it: Otteniamo il risultato:
en: We obtain the result: {0}

it: Ora ripetiamo il tutto partendo dai due numeri
en: {0}Now we repeat everything starting from the two numbers 1 and –1

it: Sfasiamo il secondo numero di ϕ ottenendo 1 e - eiϕ
en: We phase-shift the second number by ϕ, obtaining 1 and - eiϕ

it: Sommiamo e prendiamone il modulo quadrato
en: {0}{1}We add and take the squared modulus

it: Otteniamo il risultato:
en: We obtain the result: {0}

it: A questo punto sembra abbastanza chiaro che le probabilità ottenute sperime
en: At this point it seems fairly clear that the probabilities obtained experimentally can be seen as the square of the sum of two complex numbers. The factor 4 present in the results does not matter because it can be eliminated by normalising the probability distribution.

it: Ora possiamo organizzare le idee nel seguente modo.
en: Now we can organise the ideas as follows.

it: Allo stato con m90=+k associamo due numeri complessi, il numero 1 che prend
en: With the state m90=+k we associate two complex numbers, the number 1 taken from the first calculation and another number 1 taken from the second calculation. With the state m90=-k we associate the second number 1 of the first calculation and the number –1 of the second calculation.

it: quando sfasiamo lo stato m90=-k moltiplichiamo i suoi due numeri per eiϕ
en: when we phase-shift the state m90=-k we multiply its two numbers by eiϕ

it: quando sovrapponiamo gli stati, sommiamo le corrispondenti coppie di numeri
en: when we superpose the states, we add the corresponding pairs of numbers and the resulting state is represented by the sum

it: I moduli quadrati dei numeri trovati danno la distribuzione di probabilità
en: The squared moduli of the numbers found give the probability distribution of obtaining the values m0=+k and m0=-k when a measurement of m0 is performed.

it: Questi numeri si riferiscono ad una particolare grandezza fisica del sistema
en: These numbers refer to a particular physical quantity of the system, in our case m0. Each single complex number is associated with one of the possible values that the physical quantity can take, and the squared modulus of these numbers gives the probability that, on measuring, the associated value occurs.

it: Osserviamo che le coppie di numeri complessi che rappresentano lo stato del
en: We observe that the pairs of complex numbers representing the state of our system form a vector space, because they can be added and can be multiplied by a number.

it: Gli esperimenti che abbiamo descritto ci hanno portato a pensare che per rap
en: The experiments we have described led us to think that, to represent physical states, it is convenient to use complex vectors, because these entities add simply when we superpose the states.

it: L'idea di usare dei vettori complessi per rappresentare gli stati si riveler
en: The idea of using complex vectors to represent states will prove very fruitful; indeed all the laws of Quantum Mechanics are written as simple linear equations in terms of these vectors. Before proceeding with the theory it is convenient to devote a section to the algebra of complex vector spaces.

it: Richiami di algebra degli spazi vettoriali complessi.
en: A review of the algebra of complex vector spaces.

it: Preso un numero complesso c=a+ib indicheremo il suo complesso coniugato con
en: Given a complex number c=a+ib we will denote its complex conjugate by the symbol c*=a-ib (c* is read “c star”). The product cc*=a2+b2 is a positive real number and is called the squared modulus of c.

it: Consideriamo ora un vettore colonna
en: Let us now consider a column vector {0}; we can define its dual by the row vector {1}. In this way the product between the vector and its dual

it: da un numero reale positivo che viene chiamato modulo quadro del vettore.
en: gives a positive real number that is called the squared modulus of the vector.

it: Se abbiamo uno stato fisico α, indicheremo il vettore ad esso associato con il simbolo
en: If we have a physical state α, we will denote the vector associated with it by the symbol {0}

it: ed indicheremo il suo duale con il simbolo
en: and we will denote its dual by the symbol {0}

it: I simboli
en: The symbols {0} and {1} are called bra and ket respectively, because joining these names gives the word “braket”, which in English means bracket. We introduced them because they let us distinguish between rows and columns, and make very clear the reading of expressions containing matrix products.

it: Ad esempio possiamo avere
en: For example we can have

it: oppure possiamo avere
en: or we can have

it: Questi due prodotti sono completamente differenti, in un caso abbiamo un vet
en: These two products are completely different: in one case we have a row vector times a column vector and obtain a number, in the other case we have a column vector times a row vector and obtain a matrix.

it: Dati due vettori
en: Given two vectors {0} and {1}, the product

it: viene chiamato prodotto scalare tra
en: is called the scalar product between {0} and {1}.

it: Ricordiamo che nel campo reale il prodotto scalare tra due vettori reali
en: Let us recall that in the real field the scalar product between two real vectors {0} and {1} is defined without the complex conjugate:

it: Osserviamo, prima di tutto, che le due definizioni sono compatibili
en: Let us observe, first of all, that the two definitions are compatible, because the complex conjugate of a real number is the number itself a*=a. So the definition given in the complex field reduces to the one given in the real field if the vectors to which it is applied are real.

it: Tuttavia può rimanere un dubbio, perché nel campo complesso non definiamo il
en: However a doubt may remain: why in the complex field do we not define the scalar product simply without the complex conjugate

it: La risposta è molto semplice, questa definizione non gode di alcune propriet
en: The answer is very simple: this definition does not enjoy some very important properties. For example, with this definition the product of a vector with itself {0} is not necessarily a real number, so the concept of modulus is lost. Moreover, in some cases the product {1} could be zero without the vector {2} being zero, for example if

it: abbiamo
en: we have

it: invece con la definizione corretta
en: whereas with the correct definition

it: Per concludere questa digressione vediamo quali sono le proprietà fondamenta
en: To conclude this digression let us see the fundamental properties of the scalar product in a complex space:

it: per definizione diremo che due vettori
en: by definition we will say that two vectors {0} and {1} are orthogonal if their scalar product is zero

it: Teorema: se n vettori non nulli
en: Theorem: if n non-zero vectors {0} are pairwise orthogonal

it: allora questi n vettori sono linearmente indipendenti
en: then these n vectors are linearly independent, that is none of them can be obtained as a linear combination of the others.

it: Dimostrazione: per assurdo supponiamo che un vettore, ad esempio il primo
en: Proof: by contradiction, suppose that one vector, for example the first, is a combination of the others

it: moltiplichiamo scalarmente a destra per
en: we multiply scalarly on the right by {0}

it: essendo i vettori per ipotesi ortogonali, abbiamo che tutti i termini al sec
en: since the vectors are by hypothesis orthogonal, all the terms on the right-hand side are zero, hence

it: ma questo è assurdo perché i vettori sono tutti non nulli.
en: but this is absurd because the vectors are all non-zero.

it: Ora supponiamo di avere uno spazio vettoriale n dimensionale
en: Now suppose we have an n-dimensional vector space and we must choose a basis for it. As is known, one can choose any n-tuple of linearly independent vectors, but it is very convenient to choose these vectors so that they are orthogonal to each other and have unit modulus {0}. Indeed, doing so it becomes very simple to compute the components of a generic vector {1}. Let us see how:

it: Prendiamo un vettore
en: We take a vector {0} and a basis of orthogonal vectors of unit modulus

it: può essere scritto come combinazione lineare dei vettori
en: {0} can be written as a linear combination of the vectors {1}

it: per calcolare il coefficiente ck moltiplichiamo questa espressione a sinistra per
en: to compute the coefficient ck we multiply this expression on the left by {0}

it: essendo i vettori di base a due a due ortogonali si ha
en: since the basis vectors are pairwise orthogonal we have

it: essendo di modulo unitario abbiamo
en: since {0} has unit modulus we have

it: Quindi ci rimane la formula per calcolare ck
en: So we are left with the formula to compute ck

it: la componente di
en: the component of {0} along the basis vector {1} is given by the scalar product between these two vectors.

it: L'algebra degli spazi vettoriali complessi ha molti altri sviluppi di cui ci
en: The algebra of complex vector spaces has many other developments that we will deal with later. For now we have wished to introduce the scalar product because it is indispensable for formulating a first simplified version of Quantum Mechanics.

it: Ritorniamo al nostro semplice sistema.
en: Let us return to our simple system.

it: Lo stato m0=+k è caratterizzato dal fatto che se si esegue una misura di m0
en: The state m0=+k is characterised by the fact that if a measurement of m0 is performed the value m0=+k is obtained with certainty, that is the probability of obtaining m0=+k is one and the probability of obtaining m0=-k is zero.

it: Quindi questo stato può essere rappresentato dal vettore ket
en: So this state can be represented by the ket vector

it: In questa scelta abbiamo un certo grado di libertà
en: In this choice we have a certain degree of freedom; indeed the probabilities are the squared modulus of the components. So the vectors {0} or {1} are also fine, because {2}. This degree of freedom must be seen as, in classical mechanics, the choice of the reference frame is seen: it is completely indifferent which choice we make, so it is convenient to make the most convenient one.

it: Per lo stato m0=-k vale un discorso analogo
en: For the state m0=-k an analogous discussion holds

it: Osserviamo che i vettori che abbiamo scelto sono ortogonali ed hanno modulo
en: We observe that the vectors we have chosen are orthogonal and have unit modulus

it: Consideriamo ora uno stato α ottenuto da una combinazione degli stati m0=+k
en: Let us now consider a state α obtained from a combination of the states m0=+k and m0=-k

it: Possiamo dire che questo stato ha proprietà intermedie a quelle degli stati
en: We can say that this state has properties intermediate to those of the component states, in the sense that, if m0 is measured, the two possible values m0=+k and m0=-k are obtained with probabilities proportional to the terms {0} and {1}

it: Per ottenere le probabilità effettive dobbiamo normalizzare la distribuzione
en: To obtain the actual probabilities we must normalise the distribution

it: Se moltiplichiamo i due numeri α+ ed α- per uno stesso numero complesso c
en: If we multiply the two numbers α+ and α- by the same complex number c

it: abbiamo la distribuzione
en: we have the distribution

it: normalizzando otteniamo esattamente le stesse probabilità di prima
en: normalising, we obtain exactly the same probabilities as before

it: Questo è coerente con il fatto che in Meccanica Quantistica i vettori
en: This is consistent with the fact that in Quantum Mechanics the vectors

it: rappresentano la stessa combinazione degli stati base e, quindi, lo stesso s
en: represent the same combination of the basis states and therefore the same physical state. Choosing one vector or the other is completely indifferent.

it: La scelta più comoda è quella di prendere vettori normalizzati
en: The most convenient choice is to take normalised vectors, that is vectors such that {0}. In this way the probabilities are obtained directly from the squared moduli, without having to normalise

it: Quindi d'ora in poi, rappresentiamo gli stati con vettori di modulo unitario
en: So from now on we represent states with unit-modulus vectors. The coefficients of a unit-modulus state vector are given a special name: they are called “probability amplitudes”. α+ is the probability amplitude of obtaining m0=+k and {0} is the probability of obtaining m0=+k, and analogously for α- and for {1}.

it: Consideriamo ora due stati mϑ=+k e mϕ=+k, e consideriamo i vettori che li r
en: Let us now consider two states mϑ=+k and mϕ=+k, and consider the vectors that represent them

it: le grandezze αϑ+, αϑ- e αϕ+, αϕ- sono, rispettivamente, le ampiezze di prob
en: the quantities αϑ+, αϑ- and αϕ+, αϕ- are, respectively, the probability amplitudes of finding m0=+k and m0=-k when the system is in the states mϑ=+k and mϕ=-k.

it: Ma quanto vale l'ampiezza di probabilità di trovare mϑ=+k misurando mϑ su a
en: But what is the probability amplitude of finding mϑ=+k by measuring mϑ on atoms in the state mϕ=+k?

it: L'ampiezza di probabilità cercata è pari al prodotto
en: The sought probability amplitude equals the product

it: cioè è pari alla componente del vettore
en: that is, it equals the component of the vector {0} along the vector {1}.

it: La probabilità, come al solito, è data dal modulo quadro
en: The probability, as usual, is given by the squared modulus

it: Questa regola era facile da intuire, ma non si può dimostrare in base alle c
en: This rule was easy to guess, but it cannot be proved on the basis of the things we had already said. It is a fundamental principle of Quantum Mechanics, and has general validity:

it: supponiamo di avere un sistema in uno stato α e di voler sapere la probabilità
en: suppose we have a system in a state α and we want to know the probability that, measuring a quantity g, a particular value {0} occurs.

it: Prima dobbiamo prendere il ket normalizzato
en: First we must take the normalised ket {0} associated with the state α.

it: Poi dobbiamo prendere il ket normalizzato
en: Then we must take the normalised ket {0} associated with the state in which, if g is measured, the value {1} is obtained with certainty.

it: Infine dobbiamo calcolare la componente del ket
en: Finally we must compute the component of the ket {0} along the ket {1}

it: ottenendo così l'ampiezza di probabilità.
en: thus obtaining the probability amplitude.

it: La probabilità è data dal modulo quadrato
en: The probability is given by the squared modulus

it: Ora siamo in grado di scegliere le giuste componenti dei vettori
en: Now we are able to choose the right components of the vectors {0} and {1} for each angle ϑ.

it: Per le probabilità relative a
en: For the probabilities relative to {0} we have the following experimental results

it: che, ad esempio possono derivare dai vettori
en: which, for example, may derive from the vectors

it: Anche in questo caso abbiamo un certo grado di libertà
en: In this case too we have a certain degree of freedom, because we cannot directly measure the probability amplitudes, but only the probabilities. The simplest choice is the following

it: Per lo stato
en: For the state {0} we can choose

it: Il segno meno davanti al
en: The minus sign in front of {0} is necessary because the two vectors must be orthogonal {1}. Indeed we know that the probability of obtaining mϑ=+k in the state mϑ=-k is zero.

it: A questo punto, per verificare la coerenza delle cose che abbiamo detto, pro
en: At this point, to verify the consistency of the things we have said, let us try to compute the probability of finding mϑ=+k in a state mϕ=+k.

it: Analogamente si può calcolare
en: Analogously one can compute

it: Esattamente in accordo con i risultati sperimentali.
en: Exactly in agreement with the experimental results.

it: Come ultimo passo ci resta da studiare l'evoluzione temporale dei vettori di
en: As a last step it remains for us to study the time evolution of the state vectors.

it: Evoluzione temporale.
en: Time evolution.

it: Dato un sistema, supponiamo di conoscere il ket di stato iniziale
en: Given a system, suppose we know the initial state ket {0} and the “ambient conditions”. We ask what the state ket {1} will be at a generic instant t.

it: Possiamo organizzare un esperimento nel seguente modo:
en: We can organise an experiment as follows:

it: prendiamo un fascio di atomi di cui conosciamo lo stato
en: we take a beam of atoms whose state we know, we pass it through a box containing various Stern–Gerlach machines and, on exit after the time {0} needed to cross the box, we observe the state of the atoms (see fig. 12).

it: Scatola
en: Box

it: Nel predisporre la scatola dobbiamo stare attenti
en: In preparing the box we must be careful; indeed we are studying a simplified system, we have atoms of which we observe only the magnetic moment, and no other quantity such as position. So we cannot insert septa that block some beams, because the state of the atoms that strike these septa would have to be described in terms of position as well as of magnetic moment, and this would take us outside the simple scheme we are studying.

it: Possiamo considerare un esperimento che abbiamo già eseguito
en: {0}We can consider an experiment we have already carried out (Experiment number 4), whose figure we reproduce.

it: Esperimento n.4
en: Experiment no. 4

it: Supponiamo di poter ruotare la prima macchina di un angolo ϑ
en: Suppose we can rotate the first machine by an angle ϑ, so at the input we have atoms in the state . When these atoms enter the box the state is split into two components

it: dove i termini c+ e c- sono dati dai prodotti scalari
en: where the terms c+ and c- are given by the scalar products

it: Il fascio
en: The beam {0} is phase-shifted by a factor eiϕ and at the output we have the composite state

it: dove U è la matrice
en: where U is the matrix

it: Questa matrice viene detta matrice di evoluzione temporale tra gli istanti t
en: This matrix is called the time-evolution matrix between the instants t0 and t, and refers to the particular environment, that is to the apparatus that constitutes the experiment.

it: In generale per qualsiasi fenomeno esiste una matrice
en: In general, for any phenomenon there exists a matrix {0} such that, given {1}, one computes {2} with the product {3}.

it: Un'importante proprietà della matrice di evoluzione temporale è che conserva
en: An important property of the time-evolution matrix is that it preserves scalar products. That is, if we take two state vectors {0} and {1}, and take the vectors obtained from their evolution

it: i prodotti scalari tra i primi e tra i secondi sono uguali
en: the scalar products between the first pair and between the second pair are equal

it: Possiamo verificare questa proprietà per il caso particolare dell'esperiment
en: We can verify this property for the particular case of experiment 4, but first let us look at a detail of algebra:

it: Se prendiamo un vettore
en: If we take a vector {0}, its dual is {1}. If we multiply {2} by a number c we have {3}, and the dual will be {4}. Hence the dual of {5} is {6}.

it: Ora calcoliamo
en: Now we compute {0} and show that the result is equal to {1}.

it: eseguendo il prodotto scalare ed eliminando i termini nulli
en: performing the scalar product and eliminating the null terms

it: Per concludere rivediamo i principi fondamentali che abbiamo individuato.
en: To conclude, let us review the fundamental principles we have identified.

it: Principi della Meccanica Quantistica (versione semplificata).
en: Principles of Quantum Mechanics (simplified version).

it: Un sistema fisico è caratterizzato dalle sue grandezze fisiche che possono essere misurate.
en: A physical system is characterised by its physical quantities, which can be measured.

it: Quando si misura una grandezza g sono possibili diversi risultati, ed ogni risultato ha una certa probabilità di uscire. Se dalla misura si ottiene un particolare risultato
en: When a quantity g is measured, several outcomes are possible, and each outcome has a certain probability of occurring. If the measurement gives a particular result {0}, the system settles into a state such that, if the measurement of g is repeated, the value {1} is obtained with certainty. Such a state is called an eigenstate of g with eigenvalue {2}.

it: Un qualsiasi altro stato α può essere considerato come una sovrapposizione degli autostati di una grandezza g e può essere rappresentato da un vettore di numeri complessi
en: Any other state α can be considered as a superposition of the eigenstates of a quantity g and can be represented by a vector of complex numbers {0}. Each complex number is associated with one of the possible values that the quantity g can take, {1} The squared modulus of each of these complex numbers, for example α<sub>k</sub>, gives the probability that measuring the quantity g yields the particular value {2} associated with the number considered.

it: In generale se il sistema si trova in uno stato α e si misura una grandezza
en: In general, if the system is in a state α and a quantity {0} is measured, the probability that {1} occurs is given by the squared modulus of the scalar product between the vector {2} and the vector {3} associated with the eigenstate {4} of {5}. {6}

it: L'evoluzione nel tempo di uno stato αt segue una legge lineare
en: The time evolution of a state αt follows a linear law {0}

it: dove è una matrice che dipende dal sistema e dalle
en: where {0} is a matrix that depends on the system and on the “ambient conditions”. The matrix {1} is called the time-evolution matrix and has the property of leaving scalar products invariant {2}

it: Questi che abbiamo sintetizzato sono una versione semplificata dei principi
en: What we have summarised is a simplified version of the fundamental principles. In general, to describe the state of a system it is not sufficient to use a single observable quantity, but it will be necessary to use more than one. In what follows we will show how the theory is generalised to more complex cases, and we will address the important problem of determining the time-evolution matrix for the various systems we will study.
